examples.title: Hamming Code Encoder (7,4)
examples.description: Encodes 4 data bits into 7-bit Hamming codeword with parity bits at positions 1,2,4
examples.code:
FUNCTION EncodeHamming(data[4]):
 // data bits at positions 3,5,6,7
 codeword[7]
 codeword[3] = data[0]
 codeword[5] = data[1]
 codeword[6] = data[2]
 codeword[7] = data[3]
 
 // Parity bit at position 1 (covers 3,5,7)
 codeword[1] = codeword[3] XOR codeword[5] XOR codeword[7]
 
 // Parity bit at position 2 (covers 3,6,7)
 codeword[2] = codeword[3] XOR codeword[6] XOR codeword[7]
 
 // Parity bit at position 4 (covers 5,6,7)
 codeword[4] = codeword[5] XOR codeword[6] XOR codeword[7]
 
 RETURN codeword
examples.explanation: Each parity bit covers positions whose binary representation has the corresponding bit set. Position 1 (001), 2 (010), 4 (100).

examples.title: Syndrome Decoding for Error Detection
examples.description: Calculates syndrome from received vector to detect and locate errors
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateSyndrome(received[7], H[3][7]):
 syndrome[3] = {0, 0, 0}
 
 FOR i = 0 TO 2:
 FOR j = 0 TO 6:
 syndrome[i] = syndrome[i] XOR (received[j] AND H[i][j])
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 RETURN syndrome

FUNCTION DecodeHamming(received[7]):
 syndrome = CalculateSyndrome(received)
 
 // Convert syndrome to error position
 errorPos = (syndrome[2] * 4) + (syndrome[1] * 2) + (syndrome[0] * 1)
 
 IF errorPos > 0 THEN
 // Correct error by flipping bit
 received[errorPos - 1] = NOT received[errorPos - 1]
 END IF
 
 // Extract data bits from corrected codeword
 data[4] = {received[2], received[4], received[5], received[6]}
 RETURN data
examples.explanation: Syndrome is matrix product of parity-check matrix and received vector. Non-zero syndrome indicates error position directly in Hamming code.

examples.title: CRC Generation and Checking
examples.description: CRC error detection using polynomial division
examples.code:
FUNCTION CRCEncode(data[], generator[], r):
 // Append r zero bits to data
 extended[r + length(data)]
 FOR i = 0 TO length(data)-1:
 extended[i] = data[i]
 FOR i = length(data) TO length(data)+r-1:
 extended[i] = 0
 
 // Polynomial division
 remainder = PolynomialDivision(extended, generator)
 
 // Append remainder to original data
 codeword[length(data) + r]
 FOR i = 0 TO length(data)-1:
 codeword[i] = data[i]
 FOR i = 0 TO r-1:
 codeword[length(data) + i] = remainder[i]
 
 RETURN codeword

FUNCTION CRCCheck(received[], generator[]):
 remainder = PolynomialDivision(received, generator)
 
 IF AllZero(remainder) THEN
 RETURN TRUE // No error detected
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE // Error detected
 END IF
examples.explanation: CRC divides data by generator polynomial using modulo-2 arithmetic. Remainder becomes CRC bits. Receiver checks if division yields zero remainder.
